channel.title: Cancer - Prostate on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Cancer - Prostate on VitaminDWiki

item.title: 50,000 IU of Vitamin D weekly greatly decreased bone loss from prostate cancer therapy - RCT
item.description:
• High-dose vitamin D to attenuate bone loss in patients with prostate cancer on androgen deprivation therapy: A phase 2 RCT
      • VitaminDWiki – Can get 50,000 IU Vitamin D anywhere on the globe 18+ Amazon vendors say to take it weekly
      • VitaminDWiki – Bone Mineral Density and Vitamin D - many studies

High-dose vitamin D to attenuate bone loss in patients with prostate cancer on androgen deprivation therapy: A phase 2 RCT

Cancer . 2024 Mar 23. doi: 10.1002/cncr.35275 PDF is behind a paywall

item.title: Two types of Prostate Cancer – one is far more lethal
item.description:
• Genomic evolution shapes prostate cancer disease type

Genomic evolution shapes prostate cancer disease type

Cell Geonomics VOL 4, # 3, 100511, MARCH 13, 2024 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.xgen.2024.100511

Dan J. Woodcock, Atef Sahli, Ruxandra Teslo, Rosalind A. Eeles 43, Colin S. Cooper 43, David C. Wedge 43, 46

Highlights

  • Analysis of genomic data from localized prostate cancer reveals divergent evolution

  • The shift away from the canonical trajectory is characterized by AR dysregulation

  • Tumors can be classified into evotypes according to evolutionary trajectory

item.title: 1.5X more likely to die of Prostate Cancer if low vitamin D when initially diagnosed – meta-analysis
item.description:
• Association Between Pretreatment Blood 25-Hydroxyvitamin D Level and Survival Outcomes in Patients With Clinically Localized Prostate Cancer: An Updated Meta-Analysis

Association Between Pretreatment Blood 25-Hydroxyvitamin D Level and Survival Outcomes in Patients With Clinically Localized Prostate Cancer: An Updated Meta-Analysis

Nutr Cancer. 2024 Mar 13:1-9. doi: 10.1080/01635581.2024.2328378 PDF is behind $53 paywall

Feilun Cui 1 2, Yue Qiu 3, Wei Xu 3, Chen Zou 4, Yu Fan 3

Studies on the prognostic value of the blood 25-hydroxyvitamin D level have yielded controversial results in prostate cancer (PCa) patients. This updated meta-analysis aimed to evaluate the association between pretreatment 25-hydroxyvitamin D level with survival outcomes among patients with clinically localized PCa. PubMed, Web of Science, and Embase databases were searched to identify studies evaluating the association of pretreatment 25-hydroxyvitamin D level with PCSM and all-cause mortality among clinically localized PCa patients. Ten cohort studies with 10,394 patients were identified. The meta-analysis revealed that PCa patients with the lowest 25-hydroxyvitamin D levels had an increased risk of PCSM (adjusted hazard ratio [HR] 1.52 ; 95% confidence interval [CI] 1.26-1.83; p < 0.001) and all-cause mortality (adjusted HR 1.31; 95% CI 1.00-1.90; p = 0.047) compared to those with higher reference 25-hydroxyvitamin D level. Subgroup analyses based on different sample sizes, follow-up duration, and adjusted times of blood draw also exhibited a significant association of vitamin D deficiency with the risk of PCSM. Lower pretreatment level of 25-hydroxyvitamin D may be an independent predictor of reduced survival in patients with clinically localized PCa. Measuring the pretreatment blood 25-hydroxyvitamin D level can provide valuable information for risk stratification of survival outcomes in these patients.

item.title: Prostate Cancer - interview of Dr. Hollick by Baggerly - video and transcript
item.description:
• Diagnosis Prostate Cancer: An Interview with Dr. Michael Holick and Carole Baggerly
      • Transcript
      • His ongoing diary on Facebook
      • Vitamin D ha been use to reduce hot flahes, prevent Protate Cancer, etc.
      • PSA RCT problems - 2014

Diagnosis Prostate Cancer: An Interview with Dr. Michael Holick and Carole Baggerly

YouTube 48 minutes

Transcript

(Actual Start at 0:00:13)

0:02:33.6 Carole: Welcome to one of our fantastic interviews with Dr Michael Holick. Dr. Holick today is gonna talk with us about prostate cancer and what he’s done about it. Obviously, he’s got it. So we need to know more about how he has been working with it and any advice he has for those that could benefit. So Dr. Holick, why don’t you kick it off.

item.title: Prostate cancer patients in Jamaica were 5X more likely to die of PC if low vitamin D
item.description:
• Vitamin D Deficiency at Diagnosis Increases All-Cause and Prostate Cancer-specific Mortality in Jamaican Men

Vitamin D Deficiency at Diagnosis Increases All-Cause and Prostate Cancer-specific Mortality in Jamaican Men

022 Jan-Dec;29 doi: 10.1177/10732748221131225.

Donovan McGrowder 1, Marshall K Tulloch-Reid 2, Kathleen C M Coard 1, Afette M McCaw-Binns 3, Trevor S Ferguson 2, William Aiken 4, Leroy Harrison 5, Simon G Anderson 6, Maria D Jackson 3

Background: 25 hydroxyvitamin D [25(OH)D] and serum calcium have been associated with incident prostate cancer (PCa). However, there is limited data on whether these metabolites predict survival in men of African descent, a population disproportionately affected by PCa. We studied the relationship of 25(OH)D at PCa diagnosis with all-cause and cancer-specific mortality among Jamaican men and examined whether serum calcium modified any associations.

item.title: 2.5 X reduced risk of cancer in 70 year-olds by a small amount of Vitamin D, Omega-3 and exercise – RCT
item.description:
• Combined Vitamin D, Omega-3 Fatty Acids, and a Simple Home Exercise Program May Reduce Cancer Risk Among Active Adults Aged 70 and Older: A Randomized Clinical Trial
      • Vitamin D is the 3rd best way to improve your health (but the most cost-effective)

Combined Vitamin D, Omega-3 Fatty Acids, and a Simple Home Exercise Program May Reduce Cancer Risk Among Active Adults Aged 70 and Older: A Randomized Clinical Trial

Clinical Trial. Front. Aging 3:852643. doi: 10.3389/fragi.2022.852643

item.title: Prostate Cancer PSA reduced by 60 ng of Vitamin D, Vitamin B complex, Omega-3, Curcumin and diet
item.description:
• Correlative Analysis of Vitamin D and Omega-3 Fatty Acid Intake in Men on Active Surveillance for Prostate Cancer
      • References

Correlative Analysis of Vitamin D and Omega-3 Fatty Acid Intake in Men on Active Surveillance for Prostate Cancer

Urology. 2021 Jun 15;S0090-4295(21)00525-2. doi: 10.1016/j.urology.2021.04.050

Rebecca A Campbell 1, Jianbo Li 2, Lindsay Malone 3, David A Levy 4

Chart via Google Images

Objectves: To investigate the influence of targeted serum vitamin-D level and omega-6:3 fatty-acid ratio on prostate-specific antigen (PSA) level in men with prostate cancer managed with active surveillance by providing a nutritional intervention and vitamin supplementation.

item.title: BPH (PSA) decreased by Vitamin D (50,000 IU bi-weekly) – RCT
item.description:
The effect of vitamin D supplementation on the progression of benign prostatic hyperplasia: A randomized controlled trial

Clin Nutr, . 2020 Nov 7;S0261-5614(20)30609-9. doi: 10.1016/j.clnu.2020.11.005

Abolfazl Zendehdel 1, Mohsen Ansari 2, Farnaz Khatami 3, Shapour Mansoursamaei 4, Hossein Dialameh 5

item.title: Deaths from many types of Cancer associated with low vitamin D- review of meta-analyses
Breast, Prostate and Colon Cancers are still strongly associated with low Vitamin D – Grant
item.description:
Review of Recent Advances in Understanding the Role of Vitamin D in Reducing Cancer Risk: Breast, Colorectal, Prostate, and Overall Cancer

Anticancer Research 40: 491-499 (2020). doi: 10.21873/anticanres .13977

WILLIAM B. GRANT, Sunlight, Nutrition, and Health Research Center, San Francisco, CA, U.S.A.

Director, Sunlight, Nutrition, and Health Research Center, P.O. Box 641603, San Francisco, CA 94164-1603, USA. wbgrant@infionline.net

This study is an update of Evidence that Vitamin D prevents Cancer – Grant Feb 2018

item.title: Reduce Prostate Cancer Risk: 1.3 X if eat specific plants, 1.7X if add Vitamin D
item.description:
Plant Bioactives and the Prevention of Prostate Cancer: Evidence from Human Studies – Sept 2019

Nutrients. 2019 Sep 18;11(9). pii: E2245. doi: 10.3390/nu11092245.

Livingstone TL1,2, Beasy G3, Mills RD4, Plumb J5, Needs PW6, Mithen R7,8, Traka MH9.

1.7X reduction if add Vitamin D

  • The use of Vitamin D to prevent prostate cancer doubled in a decade – May 2019

  • Prostate Cancer PSA reduced by 60 ng of Vitamin D, Vitamin B complex, Omega-3, Curcumin and diet – June 2021

item.title: Prostate Cancer associated with various genes, including Vitamin D Receptor and CYP24A1
Prostate cancer risk cut in half by consuming Boron-rich food or water
item.description:
Summary by VitaminDWiki

Clearly Boron in food/water reduces risk of Prostate Cancer
Found no data showing that taking Boron will provide the same benefit
Hypothesis that fructo-boron might be a better form
Adding Vitamin D and Omega-3 also decreases the risk of Prostate Cancer

References

  • Boron and Prostate Cancer a Model for Understanding Boron Biology

    • 2007 https://doi.org/10.1007/978-1-4020-5382-5_28,

  • Boron Slashes Risk of Prostate Cancer Sept 2018

  • Dietary boron intake and prostate cancer risk. 2004 DOI: 10.3892/or.11.4.887

item.title: Men with High Omega-3 ratio 3 times less likely to have high grade prostate cancer
item.description:
• Breast Cancer reduced 20 percent by fish (Omega-3) – meta-analysis Feb 2019

  • Omega-3 reduces side effects of Cancer treatments – April 2019

  • Omega-3 prevents and treats several cancers – Sept 2017

  • Pancreatic Cancer is increasing – Vitamin D and Omega-3 should reduce the risk

item.title: Prostate cancer 3X more likely to be lethal if both low vitamin D and poor Vitamin D Binding gene
The use of Vitamin D to prevent prostate cancer doubled in a decade
item.description:
• Trends in complementary and alternative medicine use among patients with prostate cancer.
      • Increased use of Vitamin D
      • Study had been cited 14 times as of March 2024

Trends in complementary and alternative medicine use among patients with prostate cancer.

J Urol. 2019 May 15 doi: 10.1097/JU.0000000000000336

Zuniga KB1,2, Zhao S3, Kenfield SA3, Cedars B4, Cowan JE3, Van Blarigan EL3,5, Broering JM3, Carroll PR3, Chan JM3,5.

PURPOSE:

To explore prevalence and trends of self-reported complementary and alternative medicine (CAM) use among patients with prostate cancer (PCa) using the Cancer of the Prostate Strategic Urologic Research Endeavor (CaPSURETM).

item.title: Variety of Vitamin D forms treat Prostate Cancer – RCT Review
Chemo only as needed for Prostate Cancer is working well
item.description:
A CLEVER NEW STRATEGY FOR TREATING CANCER, THANKS TO DARWIN Wired Magazine

Do not try to kill Prostate Cancer, but just keep it contained - dose only when needed

Constant dosing and the cancer evolves how to avoid the chemotherapy (carpet bombing)

war analogy - constant bombing and the enemy evolves a defense - bumkers

with infrequent dosing it does not have evolutionary pressure to learn from

classic example of the math that plotted how growing populations of snowshoe hares fuel the rise of the lynx that feed on them

item.title: Rates of some newly diagnosed Cancers increased with higher Vitamin D (217,000 Danes)
Better Cancer survival if higher vitamin D a decade earlier (esp. Melanoma, Kidney, Prostate)
Prostate Cancer death 40 percent less likely if 40 ng level of vitamin D – Meta-analysis
item.description:
• Circulating vitamin D level and mortality in prostate cancer patients: a dose-response meta-analysis - Oct 2018
      • 2.4 X more likely to die of Prostate Cancer if low Vitamin D - Oct 2020

Circulating vitamin D level and mortality in prostate cancer patients: a dose-response meta-analysis - Oct 2018

Endocrine Connections DOI: https://doi.org/10.1530/EC-18-0283

Zhenyu Song 1 , Qiuming Yao 2 , Zhiyuan Zhuo 3 , Zhe Ma 4 and Gang Chen 5 chgan305@163.com
